title: cytisus albus subsp. obscurus
intro: Cytisus albus subsp. obscurus, also known as the White Broom or Scotch Broom, is a flowering legume native to Scotland and Ireland. It has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and digestive problems. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of isoflavones and other flavonoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, contributing to its numerous health benefits, including improved cardiovascular health and reduced risk of certain cancers. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional folk medicine, although its use should be carefully monitored due to potential interactions with other medications and side effects.
